sectiondef.header: Callback identifiers used as parameters to XCan_SetHandler()

briefdescription.para: Handler type for frame sending interrupt.
Handler type for frame reception interrupt.
Handler type for all other interrupts.

sectiondef.header: Register offsets for the CAN. Each register is 32 bits.

briefdescription.para: TX High Priority Buf Data Word 1.
TX High Priority Buf Data Word 2.
RX FIFO two bit ECC error.
RX FIFO one bit ECC error.
TXOL FIFO two bit ECC error.
TXOL FIFO one bit ECC error.
TXTL FIFO two bit ECC error.
TXTL FIFO one bit ECC error.
RX FIFO Not Empty Intr Mask.

memberdef.name: XCAN_IXR_ALL
memberdef.initializer: (XCAN_IXR_WKUP_MASK | \ XCAN_IXR_SLP_MASK | \ XCAN_IXR_BSOFF_MASK | \ XCAN_IXR_ERROR_MASK | \ XCAN_IXR_RXNEMP_MASK | \ XCAN_IXR_RXOFLW_MASK | \ XCAN_IXR_RXUFLW_MASK | \ XCAN_IXR_RXOK_MASK | \ XCAN_IXR_TXBFLL_MASK | \ XCAN_IXR_TXFLL_MASK | \ XCAN_IXR_TXOK_MASK | \ XCAN_IXR_ARBLST_MASK)

sectiondef.header: CAN Frame Identifier (TX High Priority Buffer/TX/RX/Acceptance Filter
CAN Frame Data Length Code (TX High Priority Buffer/TX/RX)
CAN Frame Data Word 1 (TX High Priority Buffer/TX/RX)
CAN Frame Data Word 2 (TX High Priority Buffer/TX/RX)

briefdescription.para: Reset Mask for ECC configuration register.
Reset RX FIFO ECC error counters.
Reset TXOL FIFO ECC error counters.
Reset TXTL FIFO ECC error counters.
Maximum CAN frame length in bytes.

sectiondef.header: Mask for Low 16bits and High 16 bits

briefdescription.para: Callback type for frame sending and reception interrupts.

parameterdescription.para: is a callback reference passed in by the upper layer when setting the callback functions, and passed back to the upper layer when the callback is invoked.
is a callback reference passed in by the upper layer when setting the callback functions, and passed back to the upper layer when the callback is invoked.
is a bit mask indicating the cause of the error. Its value equals 'OR'ing one or more XCAN_ESR_* values defined in xcan_l.h

briefdescription.para: Callback type for all kinds of interrupts except sending frame interrupt, receiving frame interrupt, and error interrupt.

parameterdescription.para: is a callback reference passed in by the upper layer when setting the callback functions, and passed back to the upper layer when the callback is invoked.
is a bit mask indicating the pending interrupts. Its value equals 'OR'ing one or more XCAN_IXR_* defined in xcan_l.h

briefdescription.para: This table contains configuration information for each CAN device in the system.
This table contains configuration information for each CAN device in the system.
This function initializes a XCan instance/driver.

detaileddescription.para: This function is utilized by XCan_Initialize() or XCan_VmInitialize(), depending on if Virtual Memory support is wanted or not.

The initialization entails:
Initialize all members of the XCan structure, except BaseAddress. BaseAddress should be taken care of by XCan_Initialize() or XCan_VmInitialize(), before they call this function.
Reset the CAN device. The CAN device will enter Configuration Mode immediately after the reset is finished.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCan instance to be worked on.
points to the a configuration structure the XCan instance should be associated with.

briefdescription.para: This routine is a stub for the asynchronous callbacks.

detaileddescription.para: The stub is here in case the upper layer forgot to set the handler(s). On initialization, all handlers are set to this callback. It is considered an error for this handler to be invoked.

briefdescription.para: This routine initializes a specific XCan instance/driver.

detaileddescription.para: This function should only be used when no Virtual Memory support is needed. To use virtual memory, the caller should invoke XCan_VmInitialize(). See the description of XCan_VmInitialize() for detailed information.

This initialization entails:
Search for device configuration based on the unique device ID/BaseAddress.
Initialize Base Address field of the XCan structure using the device address in the found device configuration.
Populate all other data fields in the XCan structure
Reset the device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCan instance to be worked on.
contains the base address of the device

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if initialization was successful
XST_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND if device configuration information was not found for a device with the supplied device ID/BaseAddress.

simplesect.para: In XSCT/classic flow, DeviceId is used to look up the device configuration.

briefdescription.para: This routine initializes of a specific XCan instance/driver.

detaileddescription.para: This function should only be used if Virtual Memory support is necessary. The caller is responsible for calculating the correct device base address in Virtual memory address space and passing it into this function.

This initialization entails:
Search for device configuration based on the unique device ID/BaseAddress.
Initialize Base Address field of the XCan structure using the given virtual address parameter value.
Populate all other data fields in the XCan structure.
Reset the device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCan instance to be worked on.
contains the base address of the device

is the device base address in the virtual memory address space. The caller is responsible for keeping the address mapping from VirtAddr to the device physical base address unchanged once this function is invoked. Unexpected errors may occur if the address mapping changes after this function is called.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if initialization was successful
XST_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND if device configuration information was not found for a device with the supplied device ID/BaseAddress.

simplesect.para: In XSCT/classic flow, DeviceId is used to look up the device configuration.

briefdescription.para: This function resets the CAN device.

detaileddescription.para: Calling this function resets the device immediately, and any pending transmission or reception is terminated at once. Both Object Layer and Transfer Layer are reset. This function does not reset the Physical Layer. All registers are reset to the default values, and no previous status will be restored. TX FIFO, RX FIFO and TX High Priority Buffer are also reset.

When a reset is required due to an internal error, the driver notifies the upper layer software of this need through the error status code or interrupts The upper layer software is responsible for calling this Reset function and then re-configuring the device.
The CAN device will be in Configuration Mode immediately after this function returns.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCan instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This routine returns current operation mode the CAN device is in.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCan instance to be worked on.

listitem.para: XCAN_MODE_CONFIG if the device is in Configuration Mode.
XCAN_MODE_SLEEP if the device is in Sleep Mode.
XCAN_MODE_NORMAL if the device is in Normal Mode.
XCAN_MODE_LOOPBACK if the device is in Loop Back Mode.

briefdescription.para: This function allows the CAN device to enter one of the following operation modes:

listitem.para: Configuration Mode: Pass in parameter XCAN_MODE_CONFIG
Sleep Mode: Pass in parameter XCAN_MODE_SLEEP
Normal Mode: Pass in parameter XCAN_MODE_NORMAL
Loop Back Mode: Pass in parameter XCAN_MODE_LOOPBACK.

detaileddescription.para: Read xcan.h and device specification for detailed description of each operation mode.

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XCan instance to be worked on.
OperationMode
specify which operation mode to enter. Valid value is any of XCAN_MODE_* defined in xcan.h. Please note no multiple modes could be entered at the same time.
None.
This function does NOT ensure CAN device enters the specified operation mode before returns the control to the caller. The caller is responsible for checking current operation mode using XCan_GetMode().

briefdescription.para: This function returns Status value from Status Register (SR).

detaileddescription.para: Use the XCAN_SR_* constants defined in xcan_l.h to interpret the returned value.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCan instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: The 32-bit value read from Status Register.

briefdescription.para: This function reads Receive and Transmit error counters.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCan instance to be worked on.
will contain Receive Error Counter value after this function returns.
will contain Transmit Error Counter value after this function returns.

briefdescription.para: This function reads Error Status value from Error Status Register (ESR).

detaileddescription.para: Use the XCAN_ESR_* constants defined in xcan_l.h to interpret the returned value.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCan instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: The 32-bit value read from Error Status Register.

briefdescription.para: This function clears Error Status bit(s) previously set in Error Status Register (ESR).

detaileddescription.para: Use the XCAN_ESR_* constants defined in xcan_l.h to create the value to pass in. If a bit was cleared in Error Status Register before this function is called, it will not be touched.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCan instance to be worked on.
is he 32-bit mask used to clear bits in Error Status Register. Multiple XCAN_ESR_* values could be 'OR'ed to clear multiple bits

briefdescription.para: This function sends a CAN Frame.

detaileddescription.para: This function first checks if TX FIFO is full. If not, it then writes the given frame into the TX FIFO; otherwise, it returns error code immediately. This function does not wait for the given frame being sent to CAN bus.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCan instance to be worked on.
is a pointer to a 32-bit aligned buffer containing the CAN frame to be sent.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if TX FIFO was not full and the given frame was written into the FIFO;
XST_FIFO_NO_ROOM if there is no room in the TX FIFO for the given frame

briefdescription.para: This function receives a CAN Frame.

detaileddescription.para: This function first checks if RX FIFO is empty, if not, it then reads a frame from the RX FIFO into the given buffer. This function returns error code immediately if there is no frame in the RX FIFO.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCan instance to be worked on.
is a pointer to a 32-bit aligned buffer where the CAN frame to be written.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if RX FIFO was not empty and a frame was read from RX FIFO successfully and written into the given buffer;
XST_NO_DATA if there is no frame to be received from the FIFO

briefdescription.para: This routine sends a CAN High Priority frame.

detaileddescription.para: This function first checks if TX High Priority Buffer is empty. If yes, it then writes the given frame into the Buffer. If not, This function returns immediately. This function does not wait for the given frame being sent to CAN bus.

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XCan instance to be worked on.
FramePtr
is a pointer to a 32-bit aligned buffer containing the CAN High Priority frame to be sent.
XST_SUCCESS if TX High Priority Buffer was not full and the given frame was written into the buffer;
XST_FIFO_NO_ROOM if there is no room in the TX High Priority Buffer for this frame.
If the frame needs to be sent immediately and not delayed by processor's interrupts handling, the caller should disable interrupt at processor level before invoking this function.

briefdescription.para: This routine enables individual acceptance filters.

detaileddescription.para: Up to 4 filters could be enabled.

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XCan instance to be worked on.
FilterIndexes
specifies which filter(s) to enable. Use any XCAN_AFR_UAF*_MASK to enable one filter, and "Or" multiple XCAN_AFR_UAF*_MASK values if multiple filters need to be enabled. Any filter not specified in this parameter will keep its previous enable/disable setting.
None.
Acceptance Filter Register is an optional register in Xilinx CAN device. If it is NOT existing in the device, this function should NOT be used. Calling this function in this case will cause an assertion failure.

briefdescription.para: This routine disables individual acceptance filters.

detaileddescription.para: Up to 4 filters could be disabled. If all acceptance filters are disabled then all received frames are stored in the RX FIFO.

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XCan instance to be worked on.
FilterIndexes
specifies which filter(s) to disable. Use any XCAN_AFR_UAF*_MASK to disable one filter, and "Or" multiple XCAN_AFR_UAF*_MASK values if multiple filters need to be disabled. Any filter not specified in this parameter will keep its previous enable/disable setting. If all acceptance filters are disabled then all received frames are stored in the RX FIFO.
None.
Acceptance Filter Register is an optional register in Xilinx CAN device. If it is NOT existing in the device, this function should NOT be used. Calling this function in this case will cause an assertion failure.

briefdescription.para: This function returns enabled acceptance filters.

detaileddescription.para: Use XCAN_AFR_UAF*_MASK defined in xcan_l.h to interpret the returned value. If no acceptance filters are enabled then all received frames are stored in the RX FIFO.

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XCan instance to be worked on.
The value stored in Acceptance Filter Register.
Acceptance Filter Register is an optional register in Xilinx CAN device. If it is NOT existing in the device, this function should NOT be used. Calling this function in this case will cause an assertion failure.

memberdef.argsstring: (XCan *InstancePtr, u32 FilterIndex, u32 MaskValue, u32 IdValue)
memberdef.name: XCan_AcceptFilterSet

briefdescription.para: This function sets values to the Acceptance Filter Mask Register (AFMR) and Acceptance Filter ID Register (AFIR) for the specified Acceptance Filter.

detaileddescription.para: Use XCAN_IDR_* defined in xcan_l.h to create the values to set the filter. Read xcan.h and device specification for details.

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XCan instance to be worked on.
FilterIndex
defines which Acceptance Filter Mask and ID Register to set. Use any single XCAN_AFR_UAF*_MASK value.
MaskValue
is the value to write to the chosen Acceptance Filter Mask Register.
IdValue
is the value to write to the chosen Acceptance Filter ID Register.
XST_SUCCESS if the values were set successfully.
XST_FAILURE if the given filter was not disabled, or the CAN device was not ready to accept writes to AFMR and AFIR.
Acceptance Filter Mask and ID Registers are optional registers in Xilinx CAN device. If they are NOT existing in the device, this function should NOT be used. Calling this function in this case will cause an assertion failure.

briefdescription.para: This function reads the values of the Acceptance Filter Mask and ID Register for the specified Acceptance Filter.

detaileddescription.para: Use XCAN_IDR_* defined in xcan_l.h to interpret the values. Read xcan.h and device specification for details.

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XCan instance to be worked on.
FilterIndex
defines which Acceptance Filter Mask Register to get Mask and ID from. Use any single XCAN_FILTER_* value.
MaskValue
will store the Mask value read from the chosen Acceptance Filter Mask Register after this function returns.
IdValue
will store the ID value read from the chosen Acceptance Filter ID Register after this function returns.
None.
Acceptance Filter Mask and ID Registers are optional registers in Xilinx CAN device. If they are NOT existing in the device, this function should NOT be used. Calling this function in this case will cause an assertion failure.

briefdescription.para: Looks up the device configuration based on the unique device ID/BaseAddress.

detaileddescription.para: The XCan_ConfigTable[] contains the configuration info for each device in the system.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the device

simplesect.para: A pointer to the configuration found or NULL if the specified device ID/BaseAddress was not found. See xcan.h for the definition of XCan_Config.
In XSCT/classic flow, DeviceId is used to look up the device configuration.

briefdescription.para: This function looks for the device configuration based on the device index.

detaileddescription.para: The table XCan_ConfigTable[] contains the configuration information for each device in the system.

parameterdescription.para: is a 0-based integer indexing all CAN devices in the system.

simplesect.para: A pointer to the configuration table entry corresponding to the given device ID, or NULL if no match is found.

briefdescription.para: This function resets the ECC counters.

detaileddescription.para: Calling this function resets all the ECC counters immediately. The register is reset to default value, and no previous status will be restored.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCan instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the count of ECC error count for one and two bit error in TX_TL FIFO, TX_OL FIFO, RX FIFO.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCan instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This routine sets Baud Rate Prescaler value.

detaileddescription.para: The system clock for the CAN controller is divided by (Prescaler + 1) to generate the quantum clock needed for sampling and synchronization. Read the device specification for details.
Baud Rate Prescaler could be set only after CAN device entered Configuration Mode. So please call XCan_EnterMode() to enter Configuration Mode before using this function.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCan instance to be worked on.
is the value to set. Valid values are from 0 to 255.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the Baud Rate Prescaler value is set successfully.
XST_FAILURE if CAN device is not in Configuration Mode.

briefdescription.para: This routine gets Baud Rate Prescaler value.

detaileddescription.para: The system clock for the CAN controller is divided by (Prescaler + 1) to generate the quantum clock needed for sampling and synchronization. Read the device specification for details.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCan instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: Current used Baud Rate Prescaler value. The value's range is from 0 to 255.

memberdef.argsstring: (XCan *InstancePtr, u8 SyncJumpWidth, u8 TimeSegment2, u8 TimeSegment1)
memberdef.name: XCan_SetBitTiming

detaileddescription.para: Time segment 1, Time segment 2 and Synchronization Jump Width are set in this function. Device specification requires the values passed into this function be one less than the actual values of these fields. Read the device specification for details.
Bit time could be set only after CAN device entered Configuration Mode. Please call XCan_EnterMode() to enter Configuration Mode before using this function.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCan instance to be worked on.
is the Synchronization Jump Width value to set. Valid values are from 0 to 3.
is the Time Segment 2 value to set. Valid values are from 0 to 7.
is the Time Segment 1 value to set. Valid values are from 0 to 15.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the Bit time is set successfully.

XST_FAILURE if CAN device is not in Configuration Mode.
XST_INVALID_PARAM if any value of SyncJumpWidth, TimeSegment2 and TimeSegment1 is invalid.

detaileddescription.para: Time segment 1, Time segment 2 and Synchronization Jump Width values are read in this function. According to device specification, the actual value of each of these fields is one more than the value read. Read the device specification for details.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCan instance to be worked on.
will store the Synchronization Jump Width value after this function returns. Its value ranges from 0 to 3.
will store the Time Segment 2 value after this function returns. Its value ranges from 0 to 7.
will store the Time Segment 1 value after this function returns. Its value ranges from 0 to 15.

briefdescription.para: This function runs a self-test on the CAN driver/device.

detaileddescription.para: The test resets the device, sets up the Loop Back mode, sends a standard frame, receives the frame, verifies the contents, and resets the device again.
Note that this is a destructive test in that resets of the device are performed. Refer to the device specification for the device status after the reset operation.

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XCan instance to be worked on.
XST_SUCCESS if the self-test passed. i.e., the frame received via the internal loop back has the same contents as the sent frame.
XST_FAILURE Otherwise.
If the CAN device does not work properly, this function may enter an infinite loop and will never return to the caller.
If XST_FAILURE is returned, the device is not reset so that the caller could have a chance to check reason(s) causing the failure.
Use the XCAN_IXR_* constants defined in xcan_l.h to create the bit-mask to enable interrupts.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCan instance to be worked on.
is the mask to enable. Bit positions of 1 will be enabled. Bit positions of 0 will keep the previous setting. This mask is formed by OR'ing XCAN_IXR_* bits defined in xcan_l.h.

detaileddescription.para: Use the XCAN_IXR_* constants defined in xcan_l.h to create the bit-mask to disable interrupt(s).

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCan instance to be worked on.
is the mask to disable. Bit positions of 1 will be disabled. Bit positions of 0 will keep the previous setting. This mask is formed by OR'ing XCAN_IXR_* bits defined in xcan_l.h.

detaileddescription.para: Use the XCAN_IXR_* constants defined in xcan_l.h to interpret the returned value.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCan instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This routine returns interrupt status read from Interrupt Status Register.

detaileddescription.para: Use the XCAN_IXR_* constants defined in xcan_l.h to interpret the returned value.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCan instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: The value stored in Interrupt Status Register.

detaileddescription.para: Every bit set in Interrupt Status Register indicates that a specific type of interrupt is occurring, and this function clears one or more interrupts by writing a bit mask to Interrupt Clear Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCan instance to be worked on.
is the mask to clear. Bit positions of 1 will be cleared. Bit positions of 0 will not change the previous interrupt status. This mask is formed by OR'ing XCAN_IXR_* bits defined in xcan_l.h.

briefdescription.para: This routine is the interrupt handler for the CAN driver.

detaileddescription.para: This handler reads the interrupt status from the ISR, determines the source of the interrupts, calls according callbacks, and finally clears the interrupts.

Application beyond this driver is responsible for providing callbacks to handle interrupts and installing the callbacks using XCan_SetHandler() during initialization phase. An example delivered with this driver demonstrates how this could be done.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCan instance that just interrupted.

briefdescription.para: This routine installs an asynchronous callback function for the given HandlerType:

para.preformatted: HandlerType Callback Function Type ----------------------- --------------------------- XCAN_HANDLER_SEND XCan_SendRecvHandler XCAN_HANDLER_RECV XCan_SendRecvHandler XCAN_HANDLER_ERROR XCan_ErrorHandler XCAN_HANDLER_EVENT XCan_EventHandler

HandlerType Invoked by this driver when: ----------------------- -------------------------------------------------- XCAN_HANDLER_SEND A frame transmitted by a call to XCan_Send() has been sent successfully.
XCAN_HANDLER_RECV A frame has been received and is sitting in the RX FIFO.
XCAN_HANDLER_ERROR An error interrupt is occurring.
XCAN_HANDLER_EVENT Any other kind of interrupt is occurring.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCan instance to be worked on.
specifies which handler is to be attached.
is the address of the callback function.
is a user data item that will be passed to the callback function when it is invoked.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS when handler is installed.
XST_INVALID_PARAM when HandlerType is invalid.

simplesect.para: Invoking this function for a handler that already has been installed replaces it with the new handler.

briefdescription.para: This macro checks if the transmission is complete.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCan instance to be worked on.

listitem.para: TRUE if the transmission is done (completed).
FALSE if the transmission is not completed.

briefdescription.para: This macro checks if the transmission FIFO is full.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCan instance to be worked on.

listitem.para: TRUE if TX FIFO is full.
FALSE if TX FIFO is not full.

briefdescription.para: This macro checks if the Transmission High Priority Buffer is full.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCan instance to be worked on.

listitem.para: TRUE if TX High Priority Buffer is full.
FALSE if TX High Priority Buffer is not full.

briefdescription.para: This macro checks if the receive FIFO is empty.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCan instance to be worked on.

listitem.para: TRUE if RX FIFO is empty.
FALSE if RX FIFO is not empty.

briefdescription.para: This macro checks if the CAN device is ready for the driver to change Acceptance Filter Identifier Registers (AFIR) and Acceptance Filter Mask Registers (AFMR).

detaileddescription.para: AFIR and AFMR for a filter are changeable only after the filter is disabled and this routine returns FALSE.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCan instance to be worked on.

listitem.para: TRUE if the device is busy and NOT ready to accept writes to AFIR and AFMR.
FALSE if the device is ready to accept writes to AFIR and AFMR.

memberdef.name: XCan_CreateIdValue
memberdef.initializer: ((((StandardId) << XCAN_IDR_ID1_SHIFT) & XCAN_IDR_ID1_MASK) | \ (((SubRemoteTransReq) << XCAN_IDR_SRR_SHIFT) & XCAN_IDR_SRR_MASK) | \ (((IdExtension) << XCAN_IDR_IDE_SHIFT) & XCAN_IDR_IDE_MASK) | \ (((ExtendedId) << XCAN_IDR_ID2_SHIFT) & XCAN_IDR_ID2_MASK) | \ ((RemoteTransReq) & XCAN_IDR_RTR_MASK))

briefdescription.para: This macro calculates CAN message identifier value given identifier field values.

detaileddescription.para:
StandardId
contains Standard Message ID value.
SubRemoteTransReq
contains Substitute Remote Transmission Request value.
IdExtension
contains Identifier Extension value.
ExtendedId
contains Extended Message ID value.
RemoteTransReq
contains Remote Transmission Request value.
Message Identifier value.
C-Style signature: u32 XCan_CreateIdValue(u32 StandardId, u32 SubRemoteTransReq, u32 IdExtension, u32 ExtendedId, u32 RemoteTransReq); Read the CAN specification for meaning of each parameter.

briefdescription.para: This macro calculates value for Data Length Code register given Data Length Code value.

parameterdescription.para: indicates Data Length Code value.

simplesect.para: Value that can be assigned to Data Length Code register.
C-Style signature: u32 XCan_CreateDlcValue(u32 DataLengCode); Read the CAN specification for meaning of Data Length Code.

briefdescription.para: This macro reads the given register.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the device
is the register offset to be read

simplesect.para: C-Style signature: u32 XCan_ReadReg(u32 BaseAddress, u32 RegOffset);

briefdescription.para: This macro writes the given register.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the device
is the register offset to be written
is the 32-bit value to write to the register

simplesect.para: C-Style signature: u32 XCan_WriteReg(u32 BaseAddress, u32 RegOffset, u32 Data);

detaileddescription.para: This file contains a diagnostic self-test function for the XCan driver.
This header file contains the identifiers and basic driver functions (or macros) that can be used to access the device.
This file contains functions related to CAN interrupt handling.
This file contains a configuration table that specifies the configuration of CAN devices in the system.
Functions in this file are CAN Configuration Register access related.
Functions in this file are the minimum required functions for this driver. See xcan.h for a detailed description of the driver.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a xd 04/12/05 First release 1.10a mta 05/13/07 Updated to new coding style 2.00a ktn 10/22/09 Updated to use the HAL APIs/macros. The macros have been renamed to remove _m from the name in all the driver files. 3.2 sk 11/10/15 Used UINTPTR instead of u32 for Baseaddress CR# 867425. Changed the prototype of XCan_VmInitialize API. 3.7 ht 07/04/23 Added support for system device-tree flow. 3.8 ht 12/13/23 Added support for ECC. 3.11 sp 13/01/25 Fix GCC warnings. 3.12 vlt 12/12/25 Update Doxygen comments to include SDT flow details.

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a xd 04/12/05 First release 1.10a mta 05/13/07 Updated to new coding style 2.00a ktn 10/22/09 Updated to use the HAL APIs/macros. The macros have been renamed to remove _m from the name.

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a xd 04/12/05 First release 1.10a mta 05/13/07 Updated to new coding style 3.8 ht 12/13/23 Added support for ECC.

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a xd 04/12/05 First release 1.10a mta 05/13/07 Updated to new coding style 2.00a ktn 10/22/09 Updated to use the HAL APIs/macros. The macros have been renamed to remove _m from the name. 3.8 ht 12/13/23 Modify XCan_InterruptEnable to enable the interrupts. 3.8 ht 12/13/23 Added support for ECC.

detaileddescription.para: Other driver functions are defined in xcan.h.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a xd 04/12/05 First release 1.10a mta 05/13/07 Updated to new coding style 2.00a ktn 10/22/09 Updated to use the HAL APIs/macros. The macros have been renamed to remove _m from the name. 3.8 ht 12/13/23 Added support for ECC.

detaileddescription.para: Please see xcan.h for more information.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a xd 04/12/05 First release 1.10a mta 05/13/07 Updated to new coding style 2.00a ktn 10/22/09 Updated to use the HAL APIs/macros. The macros have been renamed to remove _m from the name. 3.3 ask 08/01/18 Fixed Cppcheck and GCC warnings in can driver 3.12 vlt 01/28/26 Fixed codespell issues.
